title: Syncope on Exertion - Aortic Stenosis
chief_complaint: Three episodes of fainting during morning walks over the past month. Also feels short of breath with activity.

physical_exam.heart: Harsh systolic ejection murmur at right 2nd ICS radiating to carotids, S2 diminished

hidden_data.ecg: Left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern
hidden_data.echo: Severe aortic stenosis, valve area 0.7cm², mean gradient 55mmHg, LVEF 55%

key_learning_points: Triad: syncope, angina, dyspnea = severe AS

patient_responses.syncope: It happens when I walk uphill, I just black out
patient_responses.chest_pain: Sometimes I feel chest tightness with walking
patient_responses.past_history: I had rheumatic fever as a child

hints.exam_hint: Listen at the right upper sternal border

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed, Doctor! Severe aortic stenosis with classic triad.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is severe aortic stenosis. The harsh systolic murmur radiating to carotids is the key finding.

echo.comment: Diagnostic - shows valve area and gradients.

feedback.attending_note: Remember the triad: syncope, angina, dyspnea. Once symptoms appear in AS, prognosis worsens significantly without valve replacement.
